Job.JobDescription:
LAWRENCE HIGH is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
VOLLEYBALL HEAD COACH (High School) 2026-2027 School Year
USD 497
Details FTE: 1.0 FISCAL YEAR: 2026-27
Due to wage and hour laws, classified staff that accept a supplemental position will be paid at an hourly rate. The stipend applies to certified staff or Rule 10 only positions. Please see Additional Pay Information below.*
QUALIFICATIONS: Sport specific participation Coaching experience (preferred) Must be CPR/AED (cardio-pulmonary resuscitation/automated external defibrillator) certified or be willing to obtain and maintain certification
REPORTS TO: School Athletic Director/Athletic Coordinator School Principal
JOB GOAL: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem.
PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES OF ALL COACHES: 1. Maintain records of evaluation procedures used in selecting squad members. 2. Enforce all rules of the Kansas State High School Activities Association. 3. Maintain an accurate squad roster at all times. 4. Verify that no candidate is issued equipment or allowed to practice until he/she has completed a KSHSAA pre- participation physical exam, notarized insurance & medical release, and tryout/participation agreement forms. 5. Supervise practice sessions and maintain security of the locker room. 6. Serve as an advisor to the students on his/her squad. 7. Accompany and supervise the team to and from all events. 8. Instruct the team in the fundamentals and rules of the sport. 9. Create teaching and coaching techniques that are educationally sound. 10. Set an example of self-control and good sportsmanship for players, students, and fans to follow. 11. Maintain good public relations with the media, parents, and the public. 12. Be responsible for the care, maintenance, and inventory of equipment and supplies. 13. Work to develop continuity in the sport throughout the district. 14. Follow the athletic department and district policies and procedures. 15. Attend all required KSHSAA and district meetings. 16. Support the head coach in their program (if an assistant). 17. Communicate effectively with students, staff, and parents. 18. See that the building and facilities are secured after use. 19. Perform other duties as assigned by the head coach and/or athletic director and/or building administrator.
RESPONSIBILITIES SPECIFIC TO HEAD COACHES: 1. Have a well-defined coaching philosophy. 2. Hire and/or recommend assistant coaches to the athletic director/building administrator. 3. Supervise and evaluate assistant coaches. 4. Schedule practices and contests in conjunction with the athletic director. 5. Work with the athletic director to develop a yearly budget. 6. Be responsible for ordering, inventorying, and maintaining equipment and supplies. 7. Work with the athletic director to schedule transportation. 8. Establish criteria for earning a letter. 9. Ensure that all KSHSAA and district policies and procedures are followed.
CRITICAL SKILLS: Ability to work with students in a supervisory role. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, and parents. Ability to work effectively with others. Ability to follow written and oral directions.
*ADDITIONAL PAY INFORMATION: For the 2026-2027 school year, the supplemental pay for this position will be $4,515. For certified teachers, this amount will be paid in one lump sum on the September 20, 2026 payroll. For classified staff, the supplemental pay will be paid as an additional hourly wage spread equally over the pay periods for the duration of the season. Classified staff cannot work more than 40 hours per week between their primary position and their supplemental position(s).
TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Length of season determined by KSHSAA.
EVALUATION: Performance of this job will be evaluated in accordance with provisions of the Board’s policy on evaluation of coaching staff.
Job.CompanyDescription: Lawrence Unified Sch. Dist. coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
BASKETBALL ASSISTANT BOYS COACH (Middle School)
USD 497 Details FTE: 1.0 FISCAL YEAR: 2025-26
Due to wage and hour laws, classified staff that accept a supplemental position will be paid at an hourly rate. The stipend applies to certified staff or Rule 10 only positions. Please see Additional Pay Information below.*
QUALIFICATIONS: Sport specific participation Coaching experience (preferred) Must be CPR/AED (cardio-pulmonary resuscitation/automated external defibrillator) certified or be willing to obtain and maintain certification
REPORTS TO: Head Coach – sport specific School Athletic Director/Athletic Coordinator School Principal
JOB GOAL: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem.
PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES OF ALL COACHES: 1. Maintain records of evaluation procedures used in selecting squad members. 2. Enforce all rules of the Kansas State High School Activities Association. 3. Maintain an accurate squad roster at all times. 4. Verify that no candidate is issued equipment or allowed to practice until he/she has completed a KSHSAA pre- participation physical exam, notarized insurance & medical release, and tryout/participation agreement forms. 5. Supervise practice sessions and maintain security of the locker room. 6. Serve as an advisor to the students on his/her squad. 7. Report injuries of participants to proper school officials. Follow injury policy as explained in coach’s handbook. 8. Support and confirm to established decisions and policy decisions of a general nature that are in keeping with the established policies and procedures of the school. 9. Organize a system for uniform and equipment checkout and insure that all students are accountable at the conclusion of each season. 10. Submit practice schedules to administration and office staff.
PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES OF ASSISTANT COACHES: 1. Support the head coach and total athletic program of the Lawrence school system. 2. Attend all staff meetings called by the head coach. 3. Assist with scouting of teams. 4. Assume any duty assigned by the head coach. Some duties may include: conducting a practice drill, handling equipment, determining eligibility, working with student managers, scouting, public relations, statistics, etc. 5. Assume all responsibilities assigned to the head coach in that coach's absence.

Job.JobDescription:
Maize Unified School Dist. is looking for a Golf Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Join our coaching staff! Maize High School is seeking an Assistant Girls Golf Coach to instruct students in the fundamental skills, strategy and training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student shall receive instructions that will lead to the formation of pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence.
Essential Functions
1. Supervise students during scheduled practices 2. Stay informed on new techniques or concepts as it pertains to the assigned area of responsibility 3. Inventory and maintain equipment and uniforms 4. Be responsible for coaching and modeling good sportsmanship and citizenship as defined by KSHSAA. 5. Provide a safe, educational, competitive environment for both practices and performances. 6. Communicate effectively with the school office, media and parents 7. Maintain the physical well being of the students in all areas pertaining to the designated area of responsibility 8. Request transportation, lodging and stipend. 9. Develop and implement practice plans that are designed to improve individual and team skills, strategies and conditioning 10. Be responsible for and ensure that all athletic and school district policies are followed by student athletes, assistant coaches and yourself 11. Be responsible for the conduct of your players and coaches during practices, games and while representing Maize High School 12. All other duties as assigned
Job.Location: Maize High (MHS) 11600 W 45th St N Maize, KS 67101
Job.CompanyDescription: Maize Unified School Dist. coaching jobs

Maize Unified School Dist. is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Join our coaching staff! Maize High School is seeking an Assistant Softball Coach to instruct students in the fundamental skills, strategy and training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student shall receive instructions that will lead to the formation of pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence.
Essential Functions 1. Supervise students during scheduled practices 2. Stay informed on new techniques or concepts as it pertains to the assigned area of responsibility 3. Inventory and maintain equipment and uniforms 4. Be responsible for coaching and modeling good sportsmanship and citizenship as defined by KSHSAA. 5. Provide a safe, educational, competitive environment for both practices and performances. 6. Communicate effectively with the school office, media and parents 7. Maintain the physical well being of the students in all areas pertaining to the designated area of responsibility 8. Request transportation, lodging and stipend. 9. Develop and implement practice plans that are designed to improve individual and team performance while adhering to safety guidelines 10. Motivate student-athletes to reach their full potential by instilling a positive attitude and setting high expectations 11. Teach student-athletes the importance of teamwork, good sportsmanship, and dedication to academics 12. Evaluate student-athletes' strengths and weaknesses and make strategic decisions regarding game strategy and player positioning 13. Attend all scheduled practices and games, arriving on time and prepared with the necessary equipment and resources 14. Stay up-to-date on developments in your sport, attending coaching clinics and workshops when possible 15. Complete all training required by KSDE and the district 16. Perform additional duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or school principal
Knowledge, Skills, Abilities 1. Knowledge of the fundamentals of the sport/activity 2. Knowledge of KSHSAA rules and regulations 3. Knowledge of USD 266 policies and expectations 4. Knowledge of age appropriate activities; lesson plan requirements; stages of child development; methods of instruction and training; basic disabilities; and behavioral management strategies 5. Ability to communicate with tact and patience 6. Ability to work with diverse groups and individuals 7. Ability to solve problems and analyze issues and create action plans 8. Ability to establish and maintain constructive relationships 9. Ability to adapt to changing work priorities and to remain flexible and creative 10. Ability to work under limited supervision
Must past background check and TB test.

Job.JobDescription:
Job Description: The Athletic Coach will be responsible for recruiting players, verifying eligibility and monitoring and verifying completion of required documentation and physical readiness requirements for student athletes. The coach then strives to build a competitive team, using practice to build physical stamina, and to teach and implement strategies that will make the best use of his players to achieve a successful season.
Performance Responsibilities: o Recruit Student athletes with an eye toward creating an inclusive team that represents our students. o Communicate all necessary information regarding all conditions for eligibility. o Conduct fair and appropriate try-out activities to assemble a team. o Monitor and confirm that all selected players have completed all required paperwork and activities or trainings prior to participation. o Coordinate practices and facility usage through the office of the Athletic Director. o Maintain all records related to uniforms and/or equipment for the program. o Communicate schedules and practice times with student athletes and families. o Train staff and athletes on proper sportsmanship and monitor communication with competition officials. o Monitor student athletes for attendance and conduct on transportation to and from team activities. o Reinforce that student athletes are representing their school, and should reinforce a positive image at all times. o Attend Athletic Director meetings o Attend Parent meetings and team events as required.
Required Qualifications: o Experience coaching and supervision of Student Athletes o Ability to complete appropriate MIAA documentation and required training for coaching protocols
Job.CompanyDescription: Fall River Public Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
PURPOSE OF THE POSITION: Guide participating student athletes in the achievement of a high level of skill, discipline, sportsmanship and citizenship. To oversee and promote the athletic program in accordance with NFHS, IHSA and RPS #205 Board Policies and athletic handbook.
SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES: None
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. In addition to the requirements listed below, regular attendance is an essential function of this position.
1. Ensure that all actions are in concert with Board policies as well as the mission statement, beliefs, objectives and parameters found in the Board's strategic plan. 2. Participates in job training and professional growth opportunities in order to enhance ability to perform the essential functions of the job. 3. Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring an efficient and effective work environment. 4. Plans and communicates with assistant coaching staff regarding practice schedule and competition strategies which ensure the health, physical well-being and success of student athletes. 5. Coordinates fundraisers, athletic activity account deposits and distribution of funds in accordance with Board Policy. 6. Manages the distribution and collection of uniforms and sport specific inventory. 7. Ensures that all student athletes are in compliance with District requirements including athletic physicals, student code of conduct, athletic fees paid, academic eligibility and all other standards included in the district athletic handbook. 8. Models/instills values of sportsmanship, citizenship and teamwork. 9. Assists with the selection of assistant and volunteer coaches. 10. Properly supervises student athletes during all related activities and events. 11. Maintains individual and team statistics and records. 12. Plans and implements a seasonal awards recognition program through the building athletic office. 13. Engages in sport specific professional development education. 14. Implements programmatic instruction of sequential fundamentals to their feeder programs and works closely with the strength and conditioning coach.
QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Valid Illinois Teachers Certificate and/or Meets IHSA Criteria (Qualification of Coaches – 2.070) 2. Previous coaching experience preferred.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES: 1. Ability to demonstrate qualities aligned to our core values of Character, Communication, Collaboration and Continuous Improvement. 2. Ability to accurately record and manage information. 3. Knowledge of industry standards and new approaches. 4. Ability to work independently under broad or limited supervision. 5. Ability to establish priorities appropriately, demonstrating an understanding of the urgency of tasks and the impact of tasks. 6. Demonstrates integrity, honesty, responsibility, and loyalty in the performance of all tasks. 7. Ability to use clear, concise language to provide timely and accurate written and oral communication.
WORKING CONDITIONS: Moderate in-district travel as well as intermittent in-state and out-of-state travel. Intermittent prolonged and irregular hours of work.
OTHER: This job description describes duties and responsibilities which are representative of the nature and level of work assigned to the position. The identified duties and responsibilities are intended only as illustrations of the various types of work that may be performed. The omission of specific duties does not exclude them from the position.
The job description does not constitute an employment agreement between the school district and employee and is subject to change by the school district as the needs of the school district and requirements of the job change.
Job.Experience: Previous coaching experience preferred

Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Football
Date Posted: 12/8/2025
Location: Alan B. Shepard High School
Date Available: Immediately
Closing Date: When Position is Filled
Assistant Football Coach Position: Assistant Football Coach Reports To: Head Football Coach / Athletic Director Stipend Category: III (Assistant Coach; see contract Appendix II for placement)
Summary and Purpose: Support student-athlete development in football and assisting the Shepard High School Athletic Department mission and vision statement. Responsibilities:
Assist the head coach in teaching football fundamentals, strategies, and sportsmanship. Supervise student-athletes during practices, travel, and related events. Attend and actively participate in all scheduled practices, games (home and away), and team functions. Support the head coach in planning practices, game management, and conducting skill instruction. Monitor and enforce all eligibility, safety, and conduct requirements as established by IHSA, the school, and district policies. Chaperone and supervise athletes at all football events (including practices, games, community gatherings, etc.), maintaining discipline and promoting a positive team environment. Maintain a safe environment and report injuries promptly to appropriate staff. Complete required paperwork, attendance logs, and communicate with parents and administration as needed. Attend coach meetings and required professional development as designed by the athletic department.
Additional Expectations:
Availability to work in the off season Assist with equipment management, set-up, and breakdown for practices and meets

Job.JobDescription:
POSITION INFORMATION Vacancy: Assistant Girls' Basketball Location: Harrison Dates: 2025-2026 Season Pay: $4,727
PRIMARY FUNCTION: To develop and implement an exemplary athletic program that provides the highest quality education and growth opportunities for every student athlete.
QUALIFICATIONS: Prior coaching experience preferred.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Performance Responsibilities: A. Professional Performance To ensure full compliance with all school and school board policies and regulations. To work cooperatively with the entire staff. To relate positively to parents and other community representatives. To demonstrate professional growth. To ensure the timely and accurate completion of all required records and reports. To demonstrate proficiency in standard English, both spoken and written. To accept and fulfill responsibilities beyond the team setting as they pertain to school expectations. To assume responsibility for care of materials, equipment, and facilities. To ensure a clean, orderly, and safe environment that supports the well-being of all student athletes. B. Instructional Performance Interpersonal Relations To foster and model positive interactions among student-athletes while building supportive relationships with the school and community. To demonstrate enthusiasm for coaching and learning. To promote self-direction and self-motivation in students, empowering them to take responsibility for their learning, growth, and success. To create clearly defined standards of behavior and academic performance. Subject Matter To exhibit comprehensive knowledge of the sport or area being coached, ensuring effective instruction and guidance for student athletes. To effectively adapt instruction of strategy and fundamentals to align with the individual needs, abilities, and interests of student-athletes. Planning/Materials To develop and sustain an organized athletic program guided by well structured, long term planning. To demonstrate effective planning and organization while employing a diverse range of appropriate strategies and resources to enhance student athlete development. Techniques/Delivery To clearly convey fundamental coaching expectations to student athletes and explain their significance for personal, athletic, and academic growth. To utilize appropriate and varied coaching techniques and learning activites. To maintain an effective system of monitoring student progress and to inform appropriate persons.
ADDITIONAL POSTING INFORMATION SPECIFIC TO JOB
QUESTIONS REGARDING THIS OPPORTUNITY? If you have any questions, please e-mail Linda Burk at linda.burk@evsck12.com or Carrie Hay at carrie.hay@evsck12.com. You will receive a response within 24-48 hours.
Job.Experience: Prior coaching experience preferred

Job.JobTitle: Extra Duty: Grade 4/5 Girls Basketball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Are you passionate about making a real difference in the world? Do you believe in the power of education to transform lives? If so, we invite you to become part of our school corporation!
Why Choose EVSC? 1. Impact Lives Daily: Every day, you'll have the opportunity to inspire, guide, and empower the next generation. Your work will ripple through time, shaping not just individual lives but entire communities. 2. Innovate and Grow: We're not just teaching – we're reinventing education for the 21st century. Bring your fresh ideas and watch them come to life in our progressive learning environments. 3. Supportive Community: Join a team that feels like family. Our collaborative environment fosters lifelong friendships and professional networks.
Join us in our mission to educate, inspire, and empower. Together, we can build a brighter future – one student at a time. We are Better Together!

Job.JobTitle: Raymond Park Intermediate Middle School Lay Coach (External Candidates Only)
Job.JobDescription: Intermediate & Middle School Athletic Assistant Coach Job Description JOB TITLE: Assistant Coach of an Athletic Sport (an extracurricular assignment) FLSA CLASSIFICATION: Not applicable. Paid via stipend. WORK DAYS: Int/Middle Athletics Season follow Athletic Schedules; within the school calendar year STANDARD HOURS PER DAY: Varies and are outside of contracted hours. WORK YEAR: Int/Middle Athletics Season follow Athletic Schedules; within the school calendar year SALARY SCHEDULE & BENEFITS: The Assistant Coach is paid on the "Teachers Athletic Extracurricular" salary schedule, paid via stipend during the season. The Teacher assigned to fulfill the duties of Coach is eligible for benefits on the "Teachers" benefits schedule due to his/her employment as a teacher, not due to being responsible for these extracurricular duties. The Assistant Coach that is not a Teacher or Non-Certified employee, but is a Lay Coach, defined as non-certified employee used only in the Athletic Program, is paid during season via stipend and not eligible for benefits. REPORTS TO: The Assistant Coach working at an intermediate and middle school reports directly to the building Principal; however, the IMS Athletic Director acts as the Principal's Designee. SUPERVISES: The Assistant Coach does not supervise anyone. GENERAL SUMMARY: The Assistant Coach supports the Head Coach in all aspects of the assigned (intermediate) middle school sport and works collaboratively with school staff and the athletic department to maintain a positive and successful athletic program. The Assistant Coach helps instruct student-athletes in fundamental skills, rules, strategies, and physical training to prepare them for competition.
Job.Location: Raymond Park Intermediate and Middle School
Job.Benefits: The Teacher assigned to fulfill the duties of Coach is eligible for benefits on the "Teachers" benefits schedule due to his/her employment as a teacher, not due to being responsible for these extracurricular duties. The Assistant Coach that is not a Teacher or Non-Certified employee, but is a Lay Coach, defined as non-certified employee used only in the Athletic Program, is paid during season via stipend and not eligible for benefits.

Job.JobDescription:
Snider High School is looking for a Golf Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
This is for the 2025-2026 School Year
Pay for this position may be prorated based upon approved hire date
ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: 1. Candidate must comply with IHSAA, MSAC rules/ regulations and all FWCS policies including FWCS code of ethics.
2. Prefer candidate with high school coaching experience.
3. Must be willing and able to work effectively with students from multicultural backgrounds.
4. Candidates must demonstrate a commitment to developing positive working relations with students, parents, teachers, and administrators.
5. Candidate will be responsible to instruct and supervise all athletic practices and games.
6. Candidate must have the ability to maintain high expectations for students both academically and athletically.
7. Candidate must have the ability to utilize, organize and work with other head coaches and assistants.
8. Candidate must have or be willing to get SPB/ Minibus certification before season.
QUALIFICATIONS:
Prefer candidate who will be teaching at Snider High School for the 2025-2026 school year. If applicant is a current FWCS teacher, the teacher must fulfill their daily contracted hours before coaching. If not an FWCS teacher, candidate must be available at 3:30 Monday through Friday afternoons.
Valid Driver's License required.
PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: To perform the essential functions of this job effectively and safely, employees must meet the following physical qualifications: Communication Skills:Ability to speak clearly and hear effectively in various environments. Manual Dexterity:Adeptness in the use of hands and fingers. Postural Endurance:Sit or stand for prolonged periods, as the job requires. Flexibility:Ability to bend at the waist, kneel, or crouch to perform specific duties. Visual Acuity:Close vision ability with the capacity to adjust focus. Mobility:Freedom to sit or walk at will throughout the work environment. Strength and Stamina:Capability to lift, carry, push, or pull up to fifty (50) pounds or more. Outdoor Work:Tolerance of work outside in various weather conditions.
FWCS will reasonably accommodate individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions.
Job.Location: Snider High School - Fort Wayne, Indiana

Job.JobDescription:
POSITION TITLE: 2025-26 Athletics Youth Strength Coordinator/Coach IMMEDIATE SUPERVISOR: Director of Youth Sports and Community Programs SALARY: $960.00 ea.
GENERAL SUMMARY: The role involves overseeing a dynamic athletics, fitness program designed to foster growth for beginners weight training students. The successful candidate will create opportunities for a broad range of students to acquire and enhance the skills required to excel as student-athletes in a competitive environment. Continual success will be pursued by aligning with the district's missions and goals. This includes maintaining regular communication and coordination with teachers, parents, athletes, support personnel, and administrative staff.
DUTIES: Responsible for helping organize activities directed at increasing knowledge of overall fitness including flexibility, explosive moment, strength and endurance. Secure any additional help to implement scheduled, on-site activities in partnership with district athletics. Partner with and build relationships with high school athletic leadership and coaches in your neighborhood/partner schools. Communicate use of the facilities and all maintenance issues with the engineering staff as well as the corporation Buildings & Grounds department to ensure safe, quality playing conditions. Recruit assistance when necessary. Help manage the athletic site and infrastructure, specifically the weight room, during the school year. Run parent meetings if necessary for all sports and communicate on a regular basis with parents about changes or concerns. Help recruit and encourage participation. Help distribute and manage any paperwork for communicating, organizing and reporting. Must work with a variety of individuals and stakeholders in order to maintain and improve the programs. Understand and apply SBCSC Code of Conduct for students participating in sport and athletic activities.
QUALIFICATIONS: Certification in fitness including personal training. Coaching experience (preferred) CPR/First Aid Certified and AED Trained (preferred) Must have reliable transportation Excellent computer skills Effective leadership and oral/written communication skills Must be able to work independently and be a self-starter
WORKING CONDITIONS Must hold a valid drivers’ license and a vehicle in good working condition. Ability to lift 40 lbs., stoop, bend and stand for long periods of time on a hard surface. Must be patient with children, which can be difficult sometimes. Able to withstand changes in temperature, occasional smoke, steam and heat and work in a confined area.
PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Requires prolonged sitting, standing, and/or walking. Routinely requires physical exertion to manually move, lift, carry, pull or push heavy objects and equipment. Must work indoors and outdoors regardless of the weather Must work long and sometimes irregular hours Works in standard office and school building environments. Must be able to handle stress. Must work regularly scheduled hours.

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Coach - Assistant Coach Girls Wrestling
Job.JobDescription:
Reports to: Head Coach
General Summary: Assistant coaches are to be committed to the direction of the program and work toward the same goals set forth by the Head Coach. When coaches and assistants are in charge of their own team, they are to adhere to the same philosophies and rules that are expected of a Varsity Head Coach. Portraying a positive image and being a leader by example are responsibilities of all coaches in the Warsaw Community School program
Essential Functions:
Coaches as directed by the head coach in teaching the skills necessary for excellent achievement by WCHS athletes With the head coach, plans and schedules appropriate practices Attends all practices and contests as scheduled Attends all staff meetings called by the head coach Assists with the processing of all paperwork pertaining to the team and the total program Enforces discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; assists with the establishment of and oversees penalties for breach of such standards by individual players Assists with the development of their respective program at all levels via attending games as designated by the head coach Assists with: weight programs, conditioning workouts, open facilities, camps, and scouting as designated by the head coach Performs other reasonably-related duties as may be assigned by the head coach Maintains compliance with all WCS, WCHS, and IHSAA policies/by-laws Attends all end of season awards programs Other duties as assigned by supervisor(s)
Qualifications:
High School Diploma/GED Playing experience in high school and/or college athletics Previous coaching experience at the high school level and/or other youth sport levels
Position Type: Extra-curricular; Non-Exempt
These statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of an assistant coach.
Position Type: Part-Time
Job.Location: Warsaw Community High School - Warsaw, Indiana
Job.CompanyDescription: Warsaw Community Schools coaching jobs

The following vacancy exists in Goshen Community Schools for the 2025-26 school year:

Job.JobDescription:
Goshen Community Schools is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The following vacancy exists in Goshen Community Schools for the 2025-26 school year:
Goshen Junior High School Assistant Girls 7th Grade Basketball Coach
POSITION PURPOSE: The Assistant Coach is responsible for helping the head coach with organizing the program at the assigned school.
ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Develops a transformational coaching statement which is clearly communicated to staff, team members, and parents. Assists the head coach with instructing players in the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. Organizes and directs individual and small group practice activities/exercises as directed by the head coach. Assesses player's skills, monitors players during competition and practice, and keeps the head coach informed of the athletic performance of students. Assists with determining game strategy. Assists the head coach with supervising athletes during practices and competition. Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury. Models sportsmanship-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators. Maintains the equipment room in orderly condition and assumes responsibility for its security. Distributes equipment, supplies, and uniforms to students as directed by the head coach. Assists the head coach with submitting a list of award winners at the end of the season. Participates in special activities to include parent's night, banquets, award nights, and pep assemblies. Performs support tasks such as distributing eligibility forms, emergency data cards, and other related records. Models nondiscriminatory practices in all activities. Attends staff development meetings, clinics and other professional activities to improve coaching performance. Performs any other related duties as assigned by the Head Coach, Athletic Director, & Principal or other appropriate administrator.
REQUIREMENTS: Must be at least 18 years of age Possess a high school diploma or equivalent IHSAA required coaching classes Preference given to GCS employees
QUALIFICATIONS: Player experience Middle School or High School coaching experience preferred Experience in teaching, coaching, and sports medicine preferred. Strong communication, motivational and organization skills. Team building abilities and working in a collaborative environment. Demonstrate success in area of application.
SEASON: December-March
SALARY: Per extracurricular schedule (Group #8) and experience

Job.JobDescription: Benefits: Based on the certified or classified status of the employee Under the supervision of the Executive Director of Athletics, the Aquatics Director will manage and coordinate all Franklin Community Schools aquatics facilities usage while maintaining, communicating, and ensuring adherence to the operating policies and procedures, and special/emergency procedures to protect the patrons and facilities. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: * Act as the lead CPO for the school district. Complete all testing and operating functions associated with the middle school and high school pools in accordance with the State and County Health Departments. * Maintain a list of certified lifeguards, and create training and certification opportunities to build a pool of lifeguards. * Schedule lifeguards for all pool activities and prepare payment claim forms for lifeguards. * Work as a coach for the FCS high school or middle school programs. * Communicate regularly with patrons, handle all patron questions and concerns, and oversee program registrations. * Work with the local swim club, high school, and middle school teams to organize volunteers; assign responsibilities, establish schedules, train, and evaluate the performance of all facility volunteers including lifeguards. * Maintain, communicate, and ensure adherence to the operating policies and procedures, and special/emergency procedures to protect the patrons and its facility. * Assist in pool and timing system set up and operations for meets hosted at Franklin Community High School including any middle school, high school, and club events. This includes events from outside organizations when applicable. * Establish and communicate facility and program schedules to maximize the use and availability of the pool including the FCS Athletic Departments, FRST Swim Club, Franklin College, and P.E. Department (both MS & HS).. * Coordinate pool repair and maintenance, and maintain accurate and appropriate inventory of supplies and equipment. * Coordinate billings and fees and provide recommendations to enhance the financial standing of facilities while maintaining quality programs and safety standards. * Develop and maintain knowledge of aquatic facility management through active participation in the appropriate professional organizations and ongoing training. * Additional duties and responsibilities as assigned by the Athletic Director. Note: The omission of specific statements of duties does not exclude them from the position if the work is similar, related, or a logical assignment to the position. MINIMUM JOB REQUIREMENTS: * Bachelor's Degree in a related field; Teaching license preferred or ability to obtain. * Nationally Certified Pool Operator * Water Safety Instructor Certification * Lifeguard Certification * CPR & First Aid Certification * Lifeguard Instructor * Franklin Community Schools HS or MS Swim Coach * Thorough knowledge of pool management and safety requirements. * Strong interpersonal skills in order to address difficult situations; such as patron complaints and disciplinary action, program program funding, and personnel issues. * Must develop ongoing working relationships with a variety of patrons, community agencies, and school administration. * General business administration knowledge and experience in order to prepare appropriate budgets, assess program effectiveness, and write proposals. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES REQUIRED: * Leadership Skills * Creativity * Flexibility * Business Skills * Strategic Thinking * Sales/Marketing * Planning and Organizing * Interpersonal Skills * Verbal Communications * Develop Relationships * Presentation Skills * Flexibility * Maturity WORKING CONDITIONS AND PHYSICAL EFFORT: * Must be able to perform physical labor without restriction such as bending, lifting, stooping, and carrying objects. * Must be able to operate manual and powered tools and equipment without restriction. * Must be able to lift up to 50 pounds. * Must be able to climb ladders and/or stairs, and work at heights over 3 feet.
Job.Benefits: Based on the certified or classified status of the employee

Job.JobTitle: Junior High Girls Head Tennis Coach
Job.JobDescription: POSITION PURPOSE To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the School Board and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Has knowledge of existing system, state and league regulations; implements the same consistently and interprets them for staff. Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Established the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of the overall program. Trains and informs staff, encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation and, at season's end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. Maintains discipline, adjusts grievances and works to increase morale and cooperation. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the Athletic Director, Assistant Athletic Director or Principal. Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advises the athletic director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes of the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to the athletic office within 24 hours. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. Determines discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contracts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Participates in the budgeting function with the athletic director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning the same. Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorizes who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for preseason meetings. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program and promotes the sport outside the school through news media, little league programs, or in any other feasible manner. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, booster club, parents, officials, volunteers and fans. Presents information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and results. The employee shall remain free of any alcohol or nonprescribed controlled substance abuse in the workplace throughout his/her employment in the Corporation. Completes other job duties assigned by the Athletic Director or Building Principal. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES Knowledge of athletics, athletic programs, and physical fitness. Knowledge of applicable federal and state laws and regulations regarding school district athletic programs, safety and health, and other areas under responsibility. Ability to evaluate, develop and implement athletic programs, activities and events, and make effective recommendations related to those areas. Effective verbal and written communication skills. Ability to organize multiple tasks and conflicting time constraints. Ability to engage in self-evaluation with regard to leadership, performance and professional growth. Ability to establish and maintain cooperative working relationships with others contacted in the course of work. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor's Degree, preferably in education or a related field Valid Indiana teaching license, preferred 2+ years’ experience as a Head Coach, preferred Motor Vehicle Operator's License or ability to provide own transportation PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Ability to stand for extended periods of time. Ability to lift 50 lbs. Ability to carry 50 lbs. Ability to work at a desk, conference table or in meetings of various configurations. Ability to see for the purpose of reading laws and codes, rules and policies and other printed matter. Ability to hear and understand speech at normal levels. Ability to communicate so others will be able to clearly understand a normal conversation. Ability to operate office equipment. Ability to reach in all directions. REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION: The District will comply with all legal requirements relating to reasonable accommodation for employees and job applicants. TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Seasonal Position EVALUATION: Performance of this job will be evaluated in accordance with provisions of the Board’s policy on Evaluations of School Administrators. DISCLAIMER: The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to perform this job. The Greenfield-Central Community School Board of Trustees does not discriminate on the basis of the Protected Classes of race, color, national origin, sex (including transgender status, sexual orientation and gender identity), disability, age, religion, military status, ancestry, or genetic information which are classes protected by Federal and/or State law.
Job.Experience: 2+ years’ experience as a Head Coach, preferred

New Palestine Junior High School is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Type: Athletics / ECA/Coaching
Date Posted: 12/11/2025
Location: New Palestine Junior High School
QUALIFICATIONS: Must be CPR certified, or willing to get CPR certified. Past experience as a coach or player preferred.
DUTY DAYS: July 1, 2026- October 9, 2026
REPORTS TO: Athletic Director
PRIMARY FUNCTION: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill and appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship.
DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES: • Coaches individual participants in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved.
• Plans and schedules practices in season.
• Recommends purchase of equipment, supplies, and uniforms, as appropriate.
• Responsible for the collection, inventory, and storage of equipment and uniforms after the season.
• Oversees the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present.
• Maintains necessary attendance forms, insurance records, and similar paperwork.
• Enforces discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times, and establishes and oversees penalties for breach of such standards by individual students.
• Model the behaviors expected from student athletes and reinforce sportsmanship and proper behavior of the athlete at all times, in the regular day school program, in practice sessions, during competition and in the community at large.
• Performs other duties as assigned.

Job.JobDescription:
Lebanon Community School Corporation is looking for a Softball Assistant Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
1. Employment to begin as of: Feb. 1st 2026
2. Position Title: LHS Varsity Assistant Softball Coach
3. Education Required: College Degree and Indiana Teaching License preferred.
4. Previous Experience Required: Experience coaching at the High School Level preferred. Experience playing at the High School or college level Softball. Highly Effective Leadership, Organizational, and Communication skills required. Ability to work well with students, staff members, and community.
5. General Description of Position Duties: Able to build personal and professional relationships with administration, teachers, coaches, players and parents. Prepares, plans, responsible and well organized. Dedicated and concerned about Softball equipment, safety, sharing of athletes, academic progress and participation numbers. Encourages player development at the high school, middle school and youth league level. Provides proper instruction, teaching skills and strategies. Understands financial and budgeting process. Is knowledgeable and has an understanding of coaching and practicing rules and policies governed by the NFHS and IHSAA. Positive role model and motivator.
6. Special Skills, Training or Aptitudes Required or Desired: CPR Certified Preferred. Complete following NFHS online courses: Concussion, Heat Index Safety, Protecting Students from Abuse, Student Mental Health and Suicide Prevention, and Sudden Cardiac Arrest. Will need to meet LCSC certification requirements which includes completing the following Safe School online courses: Blood Pathogen, Child Abuse: Mandatory Reporting & Recognition and Response, Bullying: Recognition & Response, Title IX, and Gang Awareness.
7. Position Will Be At: Lebanon High School
8. For Application or Further Information, Please Contact: Phil Levine, CAA, Athletic Director/Assistant Principal Levinep@leb.k12.in.us
9. Application Period Closes: Once position is filled
An online LCSC application form must be completed.

Job.JobDescription:
For 2026-2027 school year
Job Description: Expect a/an extra-curricular instructor/sponsor/coach, it is important that the staff conduct themselves in such a way that attention is drawn not to them, but to the students participating in the activity; dress and maintain appearance in a manner befitting the dignity and importance of the activity; maintain a standard of public conduct and morality, which serves as a proper example for the youth of Hanover Community School Corporation; maintain a good coach/student relationship that emphasizes professionalism at all times; remain professional in actions and words when dealing with stressful situations that may occur while supervising students and in doing so will show each student proper respect as an individual with dignity; additional duties explained in each professional specification.

Job.JobDescription:
Cambridge Public Schools is looking for a Basketball Assistant Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Description of Service Opportunity: The Assistant Coach is directly responsible to the Head Coach, Director of Athletics, and High School Principal. The Assistant Coach will work in close collaboration with the Head Coach and will assume assignments as directed by the Head Coach. In addition, the Assistant Coach will assist in the issue, security, and upkeep of equipment; meet with the Athletic Management Team and other coaches as requested; help in ensuring that the M.I.A.A. rules and regulations are known and followed by all players and coaches within the program; participate in clinics to enhance personal knowledge of his/her particular sport; help in ensuring the safety and security of athletes during practices and games and in the locker rooms, as well as enroute to games/meets; and perform other duties as assigned by the Head Coach and/or Director of Athletics. As part of the actual coaching and supervising in a system in which all athletes are provided appropriate information and are treated respectfully, Assistant Coaches are expected to be active participants (under MIAA regulations) in the nurturing and development of the young women and young men who participate in the CRLS athletic program. Their high level of involvement means: Establishing avenues of communication with other adults to identify potential resources for the enhancement of CRLS athletics; Working with community members who are involved in youth sports in order to encourage the young women and young men to continue to participate in sports at the high school level; Developing an approach to coaching which encourages outreach to the parents of the athletes and to other adults who care about the athletes (booster clubs are one part of this outreach; Maintaining an atmosphere of balance between the academic and athletic experiences of students and closely following each student’s academic progress; Working together with student/athletes, guidance counselors, and parents in the college application and selection process; Contacting college coaches and introducing CRLS athletes to coaches in accordance with NCAA regulations; and Identifying college scholarship opportunities for athletes. Payments are submitted to the payroll office two times per season - 1st payment in the middle of the season and the 2nd payment at the end of the season.
Minimum Requirements: Bachelor's Degree or higher from an accredited college or university is preferred. Previous successful playing and/or coaching experience required. Current CPR/AED & First Aid Certification, Online Concussion Education Certification, Online Implicit Bias Education Certification, and fulfillment of the MIAA Coaches Education Requirements, and state requirements. In addition, completion of CORI Form, and fingerprinting background check is a must.
Length of Contract: July 1, 2025 to June 30, 2026
Contract Amount: Flat Rate $2081.00

Job.JobDescription:
Cambridge Public Schools is looking for a Softball Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Description of Service Opportunity: A Head coach is directly responsible to the Director of Athletics and the CRLS Principal. The coach of an individual sport must realize that any activity sponsored by CRLS Athletics is part of the total program offered by CRLS. Individuals in charge of such activities must utilize sound educational principles and goals if athletics are to be a worthwhile part of the overall school program. Some of the duties of the Head Coach shall include: Model the behaviors outlined in the National Federation Coaches Code of Ethics, especially those pertaining to sportsmanship. Have full control of the team in all matters pertaining to coaching and athletic discipline. Implement CRLS Athletic Department policies and team rules. Be responsible for the information contained in the Massachusetts Interscholastic Athletic Association Blue Book and the CRLS Student Handbook. Supervise all assistant coaches both paid and volunteered. Attend all Athletic Department and CRLS meetings that deal directly with the sport coached. Including College Recruiting nights, Facilities and Fields meetings, and Athletic Awards meetings. Stay informed of rule changes, trends, and recommendations that affect their particular sport. Be present at each practice session and at all contests of the sport coached. This includes supervision, at all times of the weight room, locker room, and shower areas until the area is secured before and after practices and contests. Scout future opponents. Develop an organized written practice plan to include adequate warm-up and cool-down procedures on a daily basis. Make sure that the facilities and equipment are safe and that safety procedures are taught and followed in all areas. Implementing game plan. Review and assist in the processing of medical forms, parent consent forms, and eligibility/progress reports. Give personal support to the entire CRLS staff, including Deans, Teachers, and the Friends of Cambridge Athletics. Establish avenues of communication with other adults to identify potential resources for the enhancement of CRLS athletics. Work with community members who are involved in youth sports in order to encourage the young women and young men to continue to participate in sports at the high school level. Develop an approach to coaching which encourages outreach to the parents of the athletes and to other adults who care about the athletes (booster clubs are one part of this outreach) Ensure your athletes are participating in the Seasonal FOCA Fundraising event. Organize and instruct clinics for the youth teams and coaches of your individual sport. Directly supervise squad members at home and away contests. Supervise all team members that are transported by school buses. This includes making sure all team members travel to and from games and practices (when applicable) by school transportation. Be responsible for the distribution and collection of the schools athletic equipment before, during and after the coaching season and compile an inventory. Communicate and report game results and information to media and Athletic Director, and if applicable return officialÕs vouchers immediately following a contest. Assist the Director of Athletics with security by not allowing keys to be handled by anyone but the coaching staff. Have an awareness of school athletic awards policy and procedures. Distribute athletic awards in accordance with school policy. Assist student-athletes in securing financial aid or college scholarships based on athletics ability. Have current knowledge of NCAA and NAIA. And recruiting policies, regulations, and admission requirements. Perform other duties/responsibilities assigned by the Director of Athletics.
Additionally, as part of the Cambridge Education Association and Cambridge Public Schools, a memorandum of understanding does require faculty and staff (including coaches) to get tested weekly (while in season) for Covid-19. Tests must be PCR, and can be done on school campus while being offered, or at an alternative location. A Covid positive test result must be shared as soon as possible.
Payments are submitted to the payroll office two times per season - 1st payment in the middle of the season and the 2nd payment at the end of the season.
Minimum Requirements: Bachelor's Degree or higher from an accredited college or university is preferred. Previous successful playing and/or coaching experience required. Current CPR Certification, First Aid Certification, Concussion Certification, and fullfillment of the MIAA Coaches Education Requirements. In addition completion of Cori test, and federal background check is a must.
Length of Contract: July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026
Contract Amount: Flat Rate $6242.00
Job.Experience: Previous successful playing and/or coaching experience required.
Job.CompanyDescription: Cambridge Public Schools coaching jobs

Description of Service Opportunity: The Junior Varsity Coach is directly responsible to the Head Coach, Director of Athletics, and High School Principal. The Junior Varsity Coach will work in close collaboration with the Head Coach and will assume assignments as directed by the Head Coach. In addition, the Junior Varsity Coach will schedule practice sessions during appropriate times and file that schedule with the Head Coach and Director of Athletics; be responsible for being appropriately dressed as a coach; attend all contests/practices/meetings; ride the bus enroute to games/meets; assist with the varsity team as needed and perform other duties as assigned by the Head Coach and/or Director of Athletics. As part of the actual coaching and supervising in a system in which all athletes are provided appropriate information and are treated respectfully, Junior Varsity Coaches are expected to be active participants (under MIAA regulations) in the nurturing and development of the young women and young men who participate in the CRLS athletic program. Payments are submitted to the payroll office two times per season - 1st payment in the middle of the season and the 2nd payment at the end of the season.
Minimum Requirements: Bachelor's Degree or higher from an accredited college or university is preferred. Previous successful playing and/or coaching experience required. Current CPR/AED & First Aid Certification, Online Concussion Education Certification, Online Implicit Bias Education Certification, and fulfillment of the MIAA Coaches Education Requirements, and state requirements. In addition, completion of CORI Form, and fingerprinting background check is a must.

Job.JobDescription:
Somerville Public Schools is looking for a Tennis Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Job Goal: As an Athletics Coach you are directly responsible to the Head Coach the Director of Athletics. The coach of an individual sport must realize that any activity sponsored by SHS Athletics is part of the total program offered by SHS. Individuals in charge of such activities must utilize sound educational principles and goals if athletics are to be a worthwhile part of the overall school program. Some of the duties of the coach shall include:
Model the behaviors outlined in the NFHS Coaches Code of Ethics, especially those pertaining to sportsmanship. Have full control of the team in all matters pertaining to coaching and athletic discipline. Implement SHS Athletic Department policies and team rules. Be responsible for information contained in the Massachusetts Interscholastic Athletic Association Handbook and the SHS Student Handbook. Attend all Athletic Department and SHS meetings that deal directly with the sport coached. Including MIAA, GBL, Coaches Associations and Athletic Awards meetings. Stay informed of rule changes, trends, and recommendations that affect their particular sport. Be present at each practice session and at all contests of the sport coached. This includes supervision, at all times, and of the weight room, locker room and shower areas until the area is secured before and after practices and contests. Develop an organized written practice plan to include adequate warm-up and cool-down procedures on a daily basis. Make sure that the facilities and equipment are safe and clean and that safety procedures are taught and followed in all areas. Perform all duties/responsibilities assigned by both the head coach and Director of Athletics Give personal support to the entire SHS staff, including school principal, school assistant principals and teachers. Establish avenues of communication with other adults to identify potential resources for the enhancement of SHS athletics; Work with community members who are involved in youth sports in order to encourage the young women and young men to continue to participate in sports at the high school level. Develop an approach to coaching which encourages outreach to the parents of the athletes and to other adults who care about the athletes (booster clubs are one part of this outreach) Ensure your athletes are participating in the seasonal fundraisers Organize and instruct clinics for the youth programs and coaches of your individual sport. Directly supervise squad members at home and away contests. Supervise all team members that are transported by school buses. This includes making sure all team members travel to and from games and practices (when applicable) by school transportation. Be responsible for the distribution and collection of the school's athletic equipment, uniforms, medical equipment and supplies during and after the coaching season. Direct completed score report forms, and official's vouchers immediately following a contest. Help the Director of Athletics maintain security by not allowing keys to be handled by anyone but the coaching staff. Have an awareness of school athletic awards policy and procedures. Distribute athletic awards in accordance with school policy. Assist student-athletes in securing financial aid or college scholarships based on athletics ability. Have current knowledge of NCAA and NIAA recruiting policies, regulations and admission requirements. Follow current procedures for completing end-of-season responsibilities.
Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree or higher from an accredited college or university is preferred. Previous successful playing and/or coaching; experience working with adolescents. Current Red Cross CPR, First Aid Certification, completion of the MIAA Coaches Education Class and other requirements by the MIAA. Terms of Employment: Seasonal / Yearly Appointment Appointments will expire unless renewed within sixty (60) days of the last regular season game
Position Reports to: Somerville High School Athletics Director/Assistant Athletics Director

Job.JobDescription:
Collins Middle School is looking for a Basketball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Middle School Intramural Basketball Coach, Salem Public Schools, Salem, MA [SY 2025-2026] INTERNAL CANDIDATES ONLY
Intramural Basketball Coaches Needed! We're gearing up for another season of intramural basketball and are looking for staff members interested in coaching. No prior coaching experience is required, just a willingness to support students, build their skills, and create a positive, team-oriented environment. Teams will typically have one practice per week and 1-2 games, with most games held on Saturday mornings at Collins Middle School. We will hold two practices before winter break—on December 5 and 19—and will provide a full schedule when we return. is a description of what we are looking for.
Duties include safety of athletes, writing and supervising workouts for various events, presence at all meets and fundraising. Previous coaching/playing experience required.
This will include: coordinating tryouts, practice planning, game management, and fundraising. The coach will also work to ensure all school policies/procedures are being followed as it relates to the athletic programs.
Preferred: Candidate should have previous experience with Basketball Experience working with youth athletics Demonstrates a high degree of energy and a genuine interest in students and their success Experience working with students & families of diverse ethnic backgrounds

Job.JobDescription:
Wakefield Memorial High School is seeking a Head Coach for our Boys Tennis program for the upcoming 2026 Spring Season.
Requirements: All candidates should have the following: MIAA Certified Coach / NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching Varsity Coaching Experience or Extensive Coaching Experience Tennis Background (playing or coaching) Excellent communication skills Passion and energy to promote Educational Athletics CPR / First Aid Certifications
Responsibilities include but are not limited to the following: Coaches individual participants in basic and advanced skills necessary for maximum individual achievement within the team setting. Provides leadership necessary to build a program ready for sustained success. Teaches, monitors and enforces appropriate standards of discipline and sportsmanlike behavior. Monitors adherence by athletes to the rules and regulations of the MIAA. Keeps up to date with any necessary training for assigned sport. Attends regular coaches meetings organized by the Athletic Director to discuss the expectations and guidelines of the Athletic Program. Creates an environment where students are supported and encouraged to participate in the athletics program. Adheres to practice schedules, student practice rules and medical compliance as required by the league.
Job.CompanyDescription: Wakefield Public Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
N. Middlesex Public Schools is looking for a Volleyball Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Varsity Boys Volleyball Coach is responsible for leading the high school boys volleyball program, emphasizing athletic skill development, teamwork, sportsmanship, and personal growth. The coach fosters a positive, inclusive, and educational environment while preparing student-athletes for success both on and off the court. This role oversees practices, matches, athlete mentorship, program administration, and collaboration with assistant and JV coaches.
Key Responsibilities Coaching & Player Development
Teach and reinforce fundamental and advanced volleyball skills including serving, passing, setting, hitting, blocking, and defensive strategies.
Develop structured practice plans emphasizing skill development, team tactics, conditioning, and mental preparation.
Provide individualized feedback to student-athletes to improve technical performance, decision-making, and confidence.
Foster a competitive and supportive team environment that encourages hard work, discipline, and respect.
Practice & Competition
Plan, organize, and conduct structured practices for the varsity team.
Manage match lineups, rotations, substitutions, and strategic adjustments.
Analyze opponents and develop match strategies to maximize team performance.
Reinforce rules, etiquette, and ethical conduct during practices and matches.
Team Culture & Mentorship
Foster a positive, inclusive, and accountable team culture.
Encourage leadership, responsibility, and academic excellence among student-athletes.
Serve as a role model demonstrating integrity, professionalism, and respect.
Support social, emotional, and athletic growth of student-athletes.
Program Administration
Oversee tryouts, athlete evaluations, and roster management for the varsity team.
Maintain communication with athletes, parents, and school staff regarding schedules, expectations, and progress.
Manage equipment, uniforms, and logistical needs for practices and matches.
Collaborate with assistant and JV coaches to ensure consistency in coaching philosophy and skill development.
Safety & Compliance
Ensure proper supervision and safety of all student-athletes during practices, matches, and travel.
Follow MIAA, NFHS, and North Middlesex School District policies.
Maintain required certifications including First Aid, CPR, and concussion training.
Promote safe warm-up, cool-down, and injury-prevention techniques.

Job.JobDescription:
N. Middlesex Public Schools is looking for a Track & Field Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Unified Outdoor Track Coach is responsible for leading the unified track program, which pairs students with and without intellectual disabilities on the same team. This role emphasizes athletic development, teamwork, inclusion, and personal growth while fostering a positive and supportive environment. The coach creates meaningful athletic experiences, promotes sportsmanship, and ensures all student-athletes are engaged, motivated, and safe during practices and competitions.
Key Responsibilities Coaching & Athlete Development Teach track and field skills including running, relays, jumping, and throwing adapted for all ability levels. Develop inclusive practice plans that promote skill development, fitness, teamwork, and enjoyment. Provide individualized instruction and support to meet the needs of each athlete. Encourage confidence, perseverance, and social interaction through athletic participation. Practice & Competition Plan and conduct structured, inclusive practices that are engaging and safe. Organize and manage participation in unified meets, ensuring all athletes have meaningful opportunities to compete. Collaborate with assistant coaches and volunteers to support athletes effectively during practices and competitions. Promote understanding of rules, teamwork, and ethical competition. Team Culture & Mentorship Foster a positive and inclusive team environment where all athletes feel valued, respected, and supported. Serve as a positive role model and mentor to student-athletes, promoting good sportsmanship, character development, and academic success. Encourage peer mentorship and support among athletes with and without intellectual disabilities. Communication & Collaboration Communicate effectively with athletes, parents/guardians, school staff, and the athletic department regarding practices, meets, and other team-related information. Collaborate with special education staff, general education staff, and other stakeholders to ensure the program meets the needs of all participants. Work closely with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, transportation, and other logistical aspects of the program. Safety & Compliance Ensure safety and supervision of all student-athletes during practices, meets, and travel. Follow North Middlesex School District, MIAA, and NFHS guidelines for unified sports. Maintain required certifications including First Aid, CPR, and concussion training. Promote proper warm-up, cool-down, and injury-prevention techniques tailored for all athletes. Qualifications Required Knowledge of track and field fundamentals and ability to adapt instruction for a diverse range of abilities. Ability to teach, mentor, and motivate student-athletes with varying skill levels and abilities. Strong communication, leadership, and organizational skills. Completion of required coaching certifications and successful background checks. Preferred Previous experience with unified sports or adaptive athletics. Familiarity with inclusive coaching strategies and techniques. Experience working with students with intellectual or physical disabilities. Ability to collaborate with volunteers, aides, and other staff to ensure a safe, positive experience. Work Environment & Schedule Practices typically held after school; competitions may include afternoons or evenings. Participation in preseason planning, skill clinics, and optional offseason development programs. Collaboration with assistant coaches, volunteers, and athletic department staff to ensure meaningful experiences for all athletes.
Job.CompanyDescription: N. Middlesex Public Schools coaching jobs

This position is for the 2026-2027 school year.
TITLE: Head Volleyball Coach (Stipend) POSTION TYPE: ACTV
Stipend amounts are based on a percentage of the base salary negotiated by DMEA. Stipends are subject to be prorated if the full season is not worked.
Stipend for this position: $6742.00 (Current amount for 25-26)
HIRING SPECIFICATIONS:
Required: 1. Valid Iowa Coaching Endorsement or Coaching Authorization 2. Strong communication, public relations, and interpersonal skills 3. Coaching and/or leadership experience
Desired: 1. Bachelor's degree in education, coaching, or related field 2. Knowledge of overall operations of an athletic program 3. First Aid and CPR 4. Knowledge of conference rules and regulations 5. Commercial Driver's License (CDL)
BASIC FUNCTION: To instruct participants in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary to realize a high degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student athlete shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulization and internalization of pride of accomplishment, self- discipline, self-confidence, and sportsmanship.
ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: 1. Serves as the leader and manager for the athletic program 2. Develops a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both individual and team development. 3. Coaches and instructs players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. 4. Follows state, regional, and district regulations governing the athletic program. 5. Establishes and maintains written standards of student behavior and provides proper supervision of athletes at all times.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: 1. Serves as the leader and manager for the athletic program 2. Develops a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both individual and team development. 3. Coaches and instructs players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. 4.
